normalization.notes: Store Phoenician text in NFC and preserve logical right-to-left order with explicit transliteration. Do not insert vowels into script text or normalize letters to Hebrew lookalikes.

segmentation.pedagogical_units: Phoenician consonant letters, alphabet names, transliteration values, matres lectionis notes, and selected inscription formula chunks
segmentation.notes: Phoenician is a right-to-left consonantal script. Vowels are usually absent from script text, so learner-facing examples must distinguish consonant skeleton, scholarly transliteration, and cautious vocalization.
